ddls:
CREATE TABLE "motors" (
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each motor */,
  "motor_model" TEXT /* Model of the motor */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the motor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the motor was installed */,
  "production_line_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production line where the motor is installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed on the motor */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the motor (e.g., active, maintenance, offline) */,
  "operating_temperature" REAL /* Current operating temperature of the motor */,
  "voltage" REAL /* Voltage at which the motor is operating */,
  "current" REAL /* Current drawn by the motor */,
  "last_error_id" INTEGER /* ID of the last error logged for the motor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("motor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_motors_production_line_id FOREIGN KEY ("production_line_id") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_motors_last_error_id FOREIGN KEY ("last_error_id") REFERENCES error_logs ("error_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_lines" (
  "line_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production line */,
  "line_name" TEXT /* Name of the production line */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the production line */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager responsible for the line */,
  "operational_status" TEXT /* Operational status of the production line (e.g., active, maintenance, offline) */,
  "last_audit_date" TEXT /* Date of the last audit or inspection of the production line */,
  "next_audit_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the next audit or inspection */,
  "line_speed" REAL /* Speed at which the production line operates */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("line_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_lines_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Full name of the manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the manager */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the manager */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the manager (e.g., production manager, maintenance manager) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the manager's last login to the system */,
  "next_training_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the manager's next training session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "motor_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each motor configuration */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the motor the configuration belongs to */,
  "microstep_resolution_id" INTEGER /* ID of the microstep resolution */,
  "excitation_mode_id" INTEGER /* ID of the excitation mode */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the configuration was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the configuration was last updated */,
  "max_current" REAL /* Maximum current the motor can handle */,
  "min_voltage" REAL /* Minimum voltage required for the motor to operate */,
  "max_voltage" REAL /* Maximum voltage the motor can handle */,
  "operating_frequency" REAL /* Frequency at which the motor operates */,
  "drive_type" TEXT /* Type of drive used (e.g., bipolar, unipolar) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_motor_configurations_microstep_resolution_id FOREIGN KEY ("microstep_resolution_id") REFERENCES microstep_resolutions ("resolution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_motor_configurations_excitation_mode_id FOREIGN KEY ("excitation_mode_id") REFERENCES excitation_modes ("mode_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_motor_configurations_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "microstep_resolutions" (
  "resolution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each microstep resolution */,
  "resolution_name" TEXT /* Name of the microstep resolution */,
  "step_division" INTEGER /* Division of a full step (e.g., 1 for full step, 2 for half step) */,
  "max_steps_per_revolution" INTEGER /* Maximum number of steps per revolution for the given resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resolution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "excitation_modes" (
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each excitation mode */,
  "mode_name" TEXT /* Name of the excitation mode */,
  "phase_description" TEXT /* Description of the phase configuration (e.g., 2 Phase, 1-2 Phase) */,
  "current_requirements" REAL /* Current requirements for the excitation mode */,
  "voltage_requirements" REAL /* Voltage requirements for the excitation mode */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "step_sequences" (
  "sequence_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each step sequence */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the motor configuration the sequence belongs to */,
  "ms1" TEXT /* Microstep 1 (MS1) value */,
  "ms2" TEXT /* Microstep 2 (MS2) value */,
  "ms3" TEXT /* Microstep 3 (MS3) value */,
  "sequence_order" INTEGER /* Order of the step in the sequence */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the step */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of each step in the sequence */,
  "repetition_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the step sequence should repeat */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sequence_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_step_sequences_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES motor_configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "real_time_monitoring" (
  "monitor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each monitoring event */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the motor being monitored */,
  "current_step_position" INTEGER /* Current step position of the motor */,
  "operating_mode" TEXT /* Current operating mode (e.g., running, paused) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status (e.g., active, error) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the monitoring event */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Real-time temperature of the motor */,
  "voltage" REAL /* Real-time voltage of the motor */,
  "current" REAL /* Real-time current drawn by the motor */,
  "torque" REAL /* Real-time torque output of the motor */,
  "speed" REAL /* Real-time speed of the motor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("monitor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_real_time_monitoring_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the motor where the error occurred */,
  "error_code" TEXT /* Error code */,
  "error_description" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the error */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the error is resolved */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the error (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "caused_by" TEXT /* Cause of the error (e.g., overheating, step loss) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the motor maintained */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, emergency) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "performed_by" TEXT /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "date_performed" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was performed */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes */,
  "estimated_cost" REAL /* Estimated cost of the maintenance */,
  "actual_cost" REAL /* Actual cost of the maintenance */,
  "parts_replaced" TEXT /* List of parts replaced during the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, operator, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login to the system */,
  "next_training_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the user's next training session */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department FOREIGN KEY ("department") REFERENCES production_lines ("line_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the resource */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource being accessed */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of the resource (e.g., motor, configuration, log) */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the resource was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of the access event */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "motor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the motor the metric belongs to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., speed, torque) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the metric (e.g., RPM, Nm) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric collection */,
  "collected_by" TEXT /* ID of the user who collected the metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of the performance metric (e.g., operational, maintenance) */,
  "metric_source" TEXT /* Source of the performance metric (e.g., sensor, manual) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_motor_id FOREIGN KEY ("motor_id") REFERENCES motors ("motor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_collected_by FOREIGN KEY ("collected_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
